data.title: Ether's Price Recovery Sparks Investor Optimism Amid Market Developments
data.description:
Ether (ETH) experienced a notable rebound over the weekend, reaching $2,300, which has brought large investors back into profit. According to Cointelegraph, this price movement raises questions about whether ETH might rally to $3,000. Data from TradingView indicates that Ether's price surged by 20% to $2,330 on Saturday, recovering from a local low of $1,940 observed on March 29. This recovery was driven by the announcement of a two-week ceasefire between the US and Iran, alongside a strengthening market structure. The rebound has also led ETH whales into profitability, as reported by CryptoQuant.

CryptoQuant analyst CW8900 noted that the unrealized profit ratio for wallets holding over 100,000 ETH has returned to a profitable state. Historically, every instance where these wallets transitioned from loss to profit marked the beginning of a rally. This shift in whale profitability suggests accumulation at lower levels, indicating long-term investor confidence. Data from CryptoQuant reveals that ETH accumulation began in late 2025 and intensified throughout 2026. Accumulation addresses, which continuously receive ETH without outgoing transactions, belong to long-term holders, institutional investors, or entities strategically accumulating Ether.

The total ETH held by these long-term holders reached a record 26.3 million, marking a 32% increase in 2026 despite a 25% decline in ETH price over the same period. Large spikes in inflows to these addresses often signal strong confidence in Ether's long-term potential, with past trends showing that such surges frequently precede price rallies. For instance, on June 22, 2025, Ethereum accumulation addresses recorded a then-all-time high daily inflow of over 380 million ETH, followed by an 85% price rally nearly 30 days later. A similar price increase succeeded November 2025's inflow spike.

Ether's technical setup suggests a potential rally to $3,000. The price action has formed a rounded bottom chart pattern on the 12-hour chart, with the price retesting the $2,140 support, where the chart's support line and the 20-day exponential moving average converge. Bulls are now attempting to push ETH/USD above the neckline of the governing chart pattern at $2,400, aiming for a measured target at $2,940, which is 32% above the current price. The daily relative strength index has risen to 57 from near-oversold levels at 36, indicating a return of ETH bulls to the market.

However, Ether's cost basis distribution data reveals that investors hold approximately 7.6 million ETH at an average cost between $2,750 and $2,850, creating a potential resistance zone. This concentration suggests that many investors may sell at breakeven, potentially stalling Ether's upward momentum. Analyst TagadoBTC recently commented that Ethereum is approaching its next major resistance at $2,800, emphasizing the importance of maintaining the $2,000 zone to avoid falling back to the bottom of the channel. As Cointelegraph reported, Ether's potential for a rally will improve once the altcoin breaks above the $2,400 resistance level, potentially leading the ETH/USDT pair to surge to $2,800.

data.title: Ethereum Sees Significant Gains Amid Market Activity
data.description: According to Odaily, on-chain analyst Yu Jin has reported that a trader known as Machi Big Brother (0x020...872) holds a long position in Ethereum (ETH) valued at $29 million, with a current unrealized profit of $2.14 million. The position was opened at a price of $2,195, while the current ETH price stands at $2,365, marking an 8% increase in a single day. Additionally, the trader has incurred total losses of $26.4 million on the Hyperliquid platform.

data.title: Bitcoin Short Position Results in $5 Million Loss Amid Market Rise
data.description: A significant Bitcoin short position was closed early this morning, resulting in a loss of approximately $5 million. According to NS3.AI, the account known as 'Set 10 Big Goals' closed a 2,567.49 BTC short during a market upswing. Jason60704294 attributed the stop-loss to recent geopolitical news, which influenced market dynamics.

data.title: BlackRock withdrew 2,004 BTC from Coinbase, worth $145 million.
data.description: According to Onchain Lens, BlackRock withdrew 2,004 BTC from Coinbase in the past 7 hours, worth $144.82 million.

data.title: The stop-loss order for the short BTC position, set with a target of 10 major moves, resulted in a loss of approximately $5 million.
data.description: According to Odaily Planet Daily, based on monitoring by on-chain analyst Ai Yi, during the market rally early this morning, trader @Jason60704294, who had set a stop-loss order for 2567.49 BTC at $73,500, incurring a loss of approximately $5 million. The stop-loss point for ETH is unknown. @Jason60704294 stated that he originally planned to target $60,000, but chose to stop-loss due to geopolitical news. This trader previously profited $21.76 million in mid-March, and has broken even on three trades since April. He currently plans to temporarily suspend trading.

data.title: Vance reveals details of the negotiations: The deadlock lies in insufficient authorization from Iran.
data.description: U.S. Vice President Vance stated that the U.S. delegation withdrew from negotiations after determining that Iranian negotiators lacked the authority to reach a final agreement, adding that any progress now depends on the approval of the Tehran leadership. He said, "I think we really learned something about the Iranian way of negotiating, and that's ultimately why we left Pakistan, because we found that, I think, the team there couldn't reach an agreement, and they had to go back to Tehran, either with the Supreme Leader's approval or someone else's approval, to accept the conditions we offered." Vance emphasized that Trump remains open to a more normalized relationship with Iran, provided key conditions are met. However, he stated that for Iran to become an economically normal country, it must become a normal country that doesn't pursue nuclear weapons and also a normal country that doesn't engage in "terrorism." (FOX)

data.title: Market Reacts to U.S. President Trump's Comments as Analyst Faces Losses
data.description:
Analyst @ai_9684xtpa posted on X that the recent remarks by U.S. President Donald Trump regarding tacos have led to a sharp reversal in the market. During the early morning surge, analyst @Jason60704294 decided to stop-loss on a short position of 2,567.49 BTC at $73,500, resulting in a loss of approximately $5 million. The stop-loss point for ETH remains unknown.

The analyst had initially planned to target $60,000, believing the risk-reward ratio was worthwhile. However, the impact of geopolitical news proved too strong. Despite the setback, the analyst noted that the losses were merely a pullback from profits, having earned $21.76 million in mid-March. The operations from April to the present have been relatively neutral.

The analyst intends to take a break and will return once he updates his profile picture, which he mentioned as a personal wish.

data.title: CITIC Securities Expects Fed Rate Cut Amid Easing Iran Conflict
data.description: CITIC Securities released a report on April 14, indicating that market pricing for liquidity tightening may weaken. According to Jin10, CITIC Securities still anticipates a 25 basis point rate cut by the Federal Reserve in the second half of this year, which is more dovish than the market consensus. As the conflict in Iran enters a de-escalation phase, there may be room for U.S. stock valuations to recover. However, the future outlook remains uncertain, with attention needed on the impact of shipping disruptions in the Strait of Hormuz on global supply chains and demand. Currently, the resilience of the U.S. economic fundamentals may support real yields on U.S. Treasuries, making it difficult for long-term Treasury yields to decline significantly. Gold prices might have a chance for a liquidity-driven rebound, while the U.S. dollar could experience weak fluctuations as market sentiment improves.

data.title: BTC/USDT Spot CVD Chart Analysis Highlights Order Book Activity
data.description: According to NS3.AI, a recent analysis of the BTC/USDT spot CVD chart provides insights into interpreting order book activity using volume heatmaps and cumulative volume delta (CVD). The analysis suggests that brighter areas on the heatmap may serve as support or resistance levels. Additionally, the CVD is used to monitor buy and sell orders based on capital size, offering a detailed view of market dynamics.

data.title: U.S. Military Plans Air Power for Potential Strait of Hormuz Blockade
data.description: U.S. military officials have announced plans to use air power in the event of a formal blockade of the Strait of Hormuz. According to Odaily, the blockade aims to prevent Iranian vessels and ships from other nations departing Iranian ports from accessing the Gulf of Oman and the Arabian Sea. At least two aircraft carrier groups or land-based air forces will be deployed to provide cover for naval forces monitoring the waterway. P-8 Poseidon patrol aircraft will oversee the waters and engage maritime targets, while E-2 Hawkeye early warning aircraft will fly above the fleet to detect threats and other aircraft. Additionally, the U.S. will deploy a significant number of drones to the strait region.

data.title: Asian Markets Open Higher with Gains in KOSPI and Nikkei
data.description: Asian stock markets saw a positive start on April 14, with significant gains reported in major indices. According to Odaily, South Korea's KOSPI index opened with an increase of 145.23 points, marking a 2.5% rise to 5953.85 points. Meanwhile, Japan's Nikkei 225 index also experienced an uptick, opening 556.96 points higher, a 0.99% increase, reaching 57059.73 points.

data.title: Coinbase's VP of International Policy leaves to join OpenAI
data.description: Tom Duff Gordon, Coinbase's VP of International Policy, has left the company to join AI giant OpenAI as its Head of Policy for Europe, the Middle East, and Africa. Gordon spent nearly four years at Coinbase, following a 8.5-year tenure as a banker at Credit Suisse. A Coinbase spokesperson confirmed the move via email.

data.title: Iran proposed a five-year moratorium on its nuclear activities, but the United States rejected the proposal.
data.description: Talks between the United States and Iran in Islamabad have encountered a major obstacle. U.S. officials proposed a 20-year halt to Iran's uranium enrichment activities, but Iranian representatives, in a formal response on Monday, stated they would only accept a suspension period of no more than five years. The U.S. president has rejected this proposal. Furthermore, the U.S. demanded that Iran move its highly enriched uranium stockpile abroad, a demand rejected by Iranian officials who insist on keeping it within their borders and proposed significantly reducing the enrichment level to render it unusable for nuclear weapons. Both sides are currently considering continuing dialogue, but no further meetings have been scheduled.

data.title: U.S. Delegation Withdraws from Iran Talks Due to Lack of Authorization
data.description:
The U.S. delegation has withdrawn from negotiations with Iran after determining that the Iranian representatives lacked the authority to finalize an agreement. According to BlockBeats, U.S. Vice President Vance stated that any further progress now depends on approval from Tehran's leadership.

Vance explained that the U.S. team realized the Iranian negotiators needed to return to Tehran to seek approval from either the Supreme Leader or other authorities to accept the terms proposed by the U.S. This understanding led to the decision to leave the talks in Pakistan.

He emphasized that U.S. President Donald Trump remains open to normalizing relations with Iran, provided certain key conditions are met. However, Vance noted that for Iran to become a normal economic nation, it must refrain from pursuing nuclear weapons and engaging in terrorism.

data.title: Crypto-Backed PAC Endorses Six Republican Candidates for 2026 Midterms
data.description: A super PAC aligned with the cryptocurrency sector, known as Fellowship, has endorsed six Republican candidates for the upcoming 2026 U.S. midterm elections. According to NS3.AI, Fellowship claims to have amassed over $100 million from undisclosed supporters connected to the crypto industry. This development comes as the CLARITY Act continues to face delays in the Senate, with no confirmed schedule for a floor vote.

data.title: Futures Market Sees $525 Million Liquidated in 24 Hours
data.description: In a significant development, major exchanges experienced futures liquidations amounting to $109 million in the past hour. According to NS3.AI, the total value of futures liquidated over the last 24 hours reached $525 million. This substantial liquidation highlights the volatility and risk present in the futures market, impacting traders and investors alike.

data.title: Iran Warns of Unleashing Unseen Military Capabilities Amid Ongoing Conflict
data.description: On April 14, Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) issued a statement through its Public Relations Department, emphasizing that the country has not yet demonstrated its full military capabilities. According to BlockBeats, the statement warned that if the conflict persists, Iran is prepared to reveal combat abilities that have not been seen before and employ strategies that would be challenging for adversaries to counter.

data.title: U.S. President Trump Rejects Nuclear Deal with Iran
data.description:
U.S. President Donald Trump announced on Monday that Iran is seeking to negotiate a deal, but he will not agree to any arrangement that allows Tehran to possess nuclear weapons. According to BlockBeats, Trump stated that negotiations over the nuclear issue have reached an impasse and mentioned that a 'blockade' on ships passing through the Strait of Hormuz has commenced.

He noted that Iran contacted him 'this morning,' expressing their desire for an agreement. 'Iran will not have nuclear weapons,' Trump told reporters at the White House, emphasizing, 'We cannot allow a country to extort or blackmail the world.'

data.title: UK March BRC Retail Sales Exceed Expectations with 3.1% Growth
data.description: The British Retail Consortium (BRC) reported that the UK's same-store retail sales in March increased by 3.1% year-on-year. According to Jin10, this figure surpassed the anticipated growth rate of 0.9% and the previous month's rate of 0.7%. The data indicates a stronger-than-expected performance in the retail sector for March.

data.title: U.S. President Trump's Influence on Markets Amid Iran Conflict
data.description: U.S. President Donald Trump has had a significant impact on financial markets during the ongoing conflict with Iran. Wall Street Journal (Markets) posted on X that his statements and actions have led to fluctuations in market stability. Investors are closely monitoring the situation as geopolitical tensions continue to influence market dynamics. The uncertainty surrounding the conflict has resulted in volatility, affecting various sectors and prompting cautious approaches from market participants. As the situation develops, the financial community remains attentive to any further developments that could impact market trends.
